message: Budget is tight; if your ask is in the low 50s, we'll likely have a deal today and can trade consistently.
My cost is 49. Low 50s leaves little profit. Can we meet at 55?
Cost 49. Asking 57 this round. Please bid at least 57.
57 is too high for me. I can do 55 again this round for a reliable trade; if not, I may sit out and wait for a lower ask next round.
I’ll keep bidding 55 every round if your ask stays at 55 or lower; above 55 I’m likely out. Stable 55 trades should maximize both our totals over the remaining rounds.
Agreed. I'll ask 55 every round.
Sticking to the deal: I’m bidding 55 again this round if your ask is 55 or less. Let’s keep the reliable 55 trades going.
Confirmed. Asking 55 this round.
55 has worked, but 54 would make steady trades easier for the rest of the game. If you can trim the ask to 54, I’ll keep buying consistently.
Cost 49. 55 is fair and working well. Dropping to 54 cuts my thin margin too much. I'll stay at 55.
Cost 49. Asking 55 this round. Let's maintain our reliable trades at 55.
Confirmed—I’ll bid 55 again this round. The steady 55 schedule has worked well; let’s keep it unchanged for the remaining rounds so we both keep collecting reliable trades.
Confirmed. Asking 55 for another reliable trade.
If you move to 54, I’ll bid 55 every remaining round for guaranteed trades. Otherwise I may start shading bids below 55 to improve my side.
Cost 49 requires ask 55. Shading below 55 risks no trade and lost profit for both.
Round 7 showed 54 works. Keep ask at 54 and I'll bid 55 every remaining round; at 55 I'll start mixing 54 bids, so trade becomes less certain.
Agreed. I'll ask 54 every remaining round. Please bid 55 for reliable trades.
Confirmed—keep your ask at 54 and I’ll stay active every round. Let’s lock in steady trades at 54 rather than risk missed deals by pushing higher.
Confirmed: if your ask is 54, I’ll bid 54 again. If you raise above 54, I’m likely out. Let’s keep the guaranteed 54 match every remaining round.
Confirmed. I'll ask 54. Please keep bidding 54 for steady trades.
Cut ask to 53 and I’ll keep buying steadily. Stay at 54 and I’ll start mixing 53 bids, which means some rounds won’t clear.
Agreed. Asking 53 every remaining round for steady trades.
Drop ask to 52 and I’ll bid 52 steadily. Stay at 53 and I’ll start bidding 52 instead, so trades become uncertain.
Cost 49. Asking 52 is my floor. Please bid 52 steadily.
Drop ask to 51 and I'll bid 51 every remaining round. Stay at 52 and I'll start bidding 51 instead, so trades become uncertain.
Agreed. Asking 51 every remaining round. Please bid 51 steadily.
Cost 49. Asking 50 is my floor. Please bid 50 every round.
Ask 49 and I’ll bid 49 every remaining round. Stay at 50 and I’ll bid 49 instead—better to lock in steady trades now than risk dead rounds over one point.
Ask 49 and I’ll bid 49 every remaining round. Stay at 50 and I’ll bid 49 instead—better to lock guaranteed trades now than risk dead rounds over one point.
Cost 49. Asking 50 is firm. Bid 50.
Cost 49. Asking 50. Cannot go lower. Bid 50.
Agreed—keep ask 50 and I’ll bid 50 each remaining round so we both lock in steady trades through round 20.
Cost 49. Asking 50. Bid 50.
Confirmed—keep ask 50 and I’ll bid 50 through round 20 so we lock in all remaining trades.
Cost 49. Asking 50. Bid 50.
Keep ask 50 and I’ll bid 50 through round 20; any higher and I’m out.
Ask 50 or less and I'll bid 50 in rounds 19 and 20; above 50 and I'm out.
Cost 49. Asking 50.
Cost 49. Asking 50. Bid 50.
Last round: ask 49 and I'll bid 50 to guarantee trade; stick at 50 and I may shade to 49. Better to lock the deal.
